rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical one-electron cathodic reduction of accumulated N-acyliminium ion 'cation pools' to generate carbon free radicals that homo-couple or add to activated olefins (e.g., methyl acrylate)
electrochemical_role: Two-step paired sequence: anodic two-electron oxidation of N-(methoxycarbonyl)pyrrolidine (or silyl-substituted precursor) at -78 °C generates and accumulates the N-acyliminium cation pool; subsequent cathodic one-electron reduction (Ep ≈ -0.85 to -0.90 V vs Ag/Ag+) of the cation gives the α-amino C-radical

key_intermediates.name: α-amino carbon radical (3) from one-electron reduction of 2
α-amino carbanion (9) from two-electron reduction of 2
dimeric radical cation (8) from radical-cation coupling
adduct radical (20) from addition of 3 to methyl acrylate
2:1 coupling byproduct (22) from reaction of 21 with 2
simply reduced pyrrolidine (1) via protonation of 9

electron_flow: Anode performs 2e- oxidation of carbamate precursor (with loss of H+ or SiMe3+) to accumulate N-acyliminium cation; cathode then delivers 1e- to the cation giving an α-amino carbon radical, which either homo-couples (possibly via radical-radical or radical-cation dimer reduction) or adds to methyl acrylate, after which the resulting electron-poor radical is reduced once more and protonated to close a formal C-H/C=C addition.

probe_reagents.name: Zn powder (chemical 2e- reductant)
probe_reagents.observation: Predominantly gives simply reduced 1 (54%) with only 16% dimer 4, indicating 2e- pathway via carbanion 9 dominates chemically

probe_reagents.name: SmI2 (chemical 1e- reductant)
probe_reagents.observation: Gives mainly 1 (10%) with little 4 (4%); supports 2e- pathway in chemical reduction vs 1e- in electrochemistry

probe_reagents.name: Methyl acrylate (radical acceptor)
probe_reagents.observation: Trapping gives adduct 19 (up to 88% with 50 equiv TfOH), supporting intermediacy of carbon radical 3

probe_reagents.name: Trifluoromethanesulfonic acid (proton source)
probe_reagents.observation: Suppresses 2:1 byproduct 22 by protonating carbanion 21; increases 19 to 88%

probe_reagents.name: Mixed cation pool (2 + 7) crossover
probe_reagents.observation: Non-selective formation of three coupling products (4, 16, 18) consistent with radical coupling but also compatible with radical-cation or anion-cation coupling

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of cation pools 2 and 7
probe_reagents.observation: Ep = -0.85 to -0.90 V for 2 and -0.75 V for 7; positive shift vs N,N-dialkyliminium (-1.95 V) attributed to electron-withdrawing CO2Me

notes: Authors favor the radical pathway (path A: 3 adds to methyl acrylate → 20 → 21 → 19) over the carbanion pathway (path B: 2e- reduction of 2 to carbanion 9, then conjugate addition). For homo-coupling, radical-radical dimerization of 3 and radical-cation coupling (3 + 2 → 8, then reduction) cannot be experimentally distinguished. Electrode choice matters: Pt cathode favors 1e- reduction (selective dimer 4, up to 75%), while C cathode allows more 2e- over-reduction to 1. Chemical reductants (Zn, SmI2) instead favor the 2e- carbanion pathway, possibly via metal-ion stabilization of 9.
